text: Temporal Patterns of Post-operative Emergencies Time Period Primary Emergencies Risk Factors Outcomes Early (024 hours) Major bleeding, Surgical complexity, Early deaths rare; hypotension, myocardial comorbidities complications often infarction, respiratory managed in-hospital depression Intermediate (24-72 Congestive heart failure, Age, cardiac history, Increased risk of organ hours) pulmonary embolism, fluid shifts dysfunction respiratory failure, acute kidney injury Late (more than 72 Sepsis, pneumonia, Infection, prior 73% of deaths occur hours) surgical site infection, complications, discharge = more than 7 days stroke, renal failure status post-operation; high post-discharge mortality
